agentic.label: Agentic AI — Research & Engineering
agentic.summary: ML researcher and engineer with hands-on experience building autonomous agents and tool-using LLM systems. Currently leading agent development at a biotech startup — designing multi-turn interaction architectures, tool-use frameworks, and execution engines for LLM-powered agents. Prior work spans multi-agent simulation, agent-based modelling calibrated to real data, and reinforcement learning in complex environments. Published at NeurIPS, ICLR, UAI (best paper), and AISTATS. Area Chair at NeurIPS 2024–25.

bioml.label: Bio-ML / AI for Life Sciences
bioml.summary: AI researcher and engineer with deep experience applying machine learning to biological challenges. Trained the diffusion model behind Latent-X for de-novo protein binder design (nanomolar affinity). Built perturbation prediction models and biological foundation models at Altos Labs. Published at NeurIPS, ICLR, and UAI.

diffusion.label: Diffusion & Generative Models
diffusion.summary: Generative modelling researcher and engineer. Trained the diffusion model behind the Latent-X platform for de-novo protein binder design, and developed improved inference algorithms that drastically increased sample quality. Prior work on diffusion models spans protein structure, rotational alignment on SO(3), and anomaly detection. Published at NeurIPS, ICLR, and UAI with best paper awards.

sbi.label: Simulation-based Inference & Bayesian ML
sbi.summary: Bayesian statistician and ML researcher specialising in simulation-based inference, MCMC, and likelihood-free methods. DPhil from Oxford under Arnaud Doucet and George Deligiannidis, with publications in Biometrika, NeurIPS, ICLR, UAI (best paper), and AISTATS. Work spans robust neural posterior estimation, approximate Bayesian computation with path signatures, and large-sample asymptotics of pseudo-marginal methods.
